role_delineation_check: The Social Worker coordinates the home safety evaluation and grab bar installation with the vendor, whereas the Patient Navigator monitors the assessment status via the Atlantis portal, documents the family's readiness sentiment, and flags any timeline shifts to the Social Worker to ensure the first visit remains synchronized with the discharge.
narrative_summary: A 68-year-old female recovering from an MS flare faced a sudden delay when her home health agency flagged a pending home safety assessment for grab bar installation. This administrative stall created significant anxiety for her daughter, who was unsure if the home was prepared for her mother's return. The Patient Navigator served as a supportive liaison by documenting the family's concerns in Atlantis, providing status updates via the Social Worker, and ensuring the daughter recognized the team's caller ID to facilitate a smooth first-day transition.
demographic_audit_note: Audited the Atlantis face sheet against the facility record; identified that the patient's primary contact number was listed as her landline, which is currently disconnected. Updated the record in Atlantis with the daughter's mobile number after verifying with the Social Worker to ensure the HHA and MA can reach the family.
v_card_flyer_status: During a bedside visit, the V-Card was successfully saved to the daughter's smartphone. I explained that 'Healing Partners' would appear on her screen for the 24-hour pre-discharge pulse call, ensuring she wouldn't mistake the coordination call for a telemarketer while managing her mother's care transitions.
pre_dc_pulse_call: Conducted the pulse call 24 hours prior to discharge. The daughter reported a Sentiment Score of 8 (High Anxiety), specifically regarding the grab bar installation. I documented this sentiment in Atlantis and flagged the training gap regarding transfer safety to the Social Worker for a quick bedside review before the patient left the building.
atlantis_final_sync: Confirmed the discharge date for Friday morning and verified via the portal that the HHA has assigned a nurse for the Saturday start-of-care. Scheduled the Medical Assistant for the first visit on Saturday afternoon and logged all time windows in Atlantis.

format_1_state_log.event_description: The patient's MS flare symptoms stabilized, but the PT evaluation noted a high fall risk, triggering a requirement for a home safety assessment before the HHA would accept the case.
The HHA portal status remained 'Pending' as the vendor struggled to find an available technician to verify grab bar placement in the patient's bathroom.
The daughter expressed significant hesitation about taking her mother home without the bars installed, threatening to pivot to a long-term care stay out of fear.
The Social Worker confirmed the assessment was completed and grab bars were installed; the HHA updated the portal to 'Accepted' with a Friday discharge target.

format_2_triples.situation: The HHA portal showed no update 48 hours after the safety assessment was ordered.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Maintained a granular wait-status timeline in Atlantis and flagged the stall to the Social Worker.

format_2_triples.situation: The family was unaware of what to do if the HHA nurse didn't show up on Day 1.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Educated the family on Day 1 expectations and provided the appointment card with the Care Coordinator's contact info.

format_2_triples.situation: The daughter was confused about whether the grab bars were already installed.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Asked the Social Worker for the final assessment report and documented the confirmation in Atlantis for the home team.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to provide a verbal update on the safety assessment before updating the patient's status in Atlantis.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This respects the SW's role as the primary coordinator for facility-ordered assessments and avoids redundant communication with the family.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Verify the safety assessment status via the HHA portal, document the family's anxiety levels in Atlantis, and flag the pending status to the Social Worker while educating the family on the transition timeline.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: This uses available digital tools to track progress and keeps the SW informed of family sentiment without the PN contacting external vendors directly.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Call the home safety assessment vendor directly to see if they can move the grab bar installation to an earlier time slot to prevent a discharge delay.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: While this might speed up the discharge, it violates the 'No-Vendor' rule and bypasses the Social Worker's authority over the discharge logistics.
